items.question: Complete relevant info added to CAD

global_guidance.YES: Requirement clearly met according to Addendum 3 intent.
global_guidance.NO: Requirement not met or insufficiently addressed.
global_guidance.REFUSED: Caller explicitly refused to answer after being asked.
global_guidance.NA: Clearly not applicable to this incident (do not use NA to excuse missing work).
global_guidance.cad_vs_interview: Interview Questions are judged on the call-taker’s questioning and the caller’s responses in the transcript. CAD Skills are judged on what was documented in the call record (tool_calls / CAD). Telephone Protocol/Skills cover tone, control, reassurance, and adherence to procedures.
global_guidance.rationale_length: Keep each rationale to 1–2 sentences citing the relevant turn(s) or record entries.
global_guidance.points_rule: Award between 0 and the item’s points value based on adequacy; partial credit is allowed.

sections.name: Interview Questions (Fire)
sections.section_guidance: Did the call-taker solicit and obtain the critical information listed? Judge by transcript content.

items.question: Verified address / location of the fire
items.guidance: Caller’s location must be obtained AND confirmed (repeat-back or explicit verification).
yes_examples: CT: "Confirm the address is 2421 Main Street?" Caller: "Yes."
Caller gives location; CT repeats it back and records it.
no_examples: Caller blurts address once; CT never confirms or acknowledges.
CT assumes location from caller’s hint without verification.

items.question: Correct fire type coded (structure, wildland, etc.)
items.guidance: The problem type should match what the caller describes (e.g., structure fire vs. vehicle fire).
yes_examples: Caller reports flames from a house; CT codes as 'Residential Structure Fire'.
Smoke in a field; CT codes wildland/grass fire appropriately.
no_examples: House on fire coded as 'Other' or 'Medical'.
No incident type entered or clearly wrong code.

items.question: Caller name obtained
items.guidance: Ask for and document the caller’s name. Nickname is acceptable if caller refuses full name.
yes_examples: CT: "What is your name?" Caller: "Jane Thompson."
Caller initially hesitant, CT gently asks again and records 'Unknown' only after refusal.
no_examples: CT accepts 'I’d rather not say' without documenting refusal (mark REFUSED instead of YES).

items.question: Callback phone number obtained/verified
items.guidance: A callback number must be asked for and either provided or refused.
yes_examples: CT: "What’s a good number to reach you?" Caller provides number and CT confirms digits.
Number auto-populated but CT verifies it verbally.
no_examples: Number given but CT never confirms and it’s clearly incomplete.

items.question: Asked if anyone is inside/trapped
items.guidance: Explicitly determine if people are inside or accounted for. Addendum 3 treats this as critical life-safety info.
yes_examples: CT: "Is anyone still inside the building?" Caller: "I think two people are upstairs!"
CT clarifies unknown status: "You’re not sure if anyone is inside, correct?"
no_examples: Assumes 'no one inside' from caller panic without asking.

items.question: Asked about exposures/adjacent structures/vehicles
items.guidance: Assess risk to nearby structures/vehicles (exposures) when applicable.
yes_examples: CT: "Are there buildings or cars close to the fire that could catch?"
CT asks if flames are near propane tanks on the porch next door.
no_examples: Large urban setting with obvious exposures but CT never inquires.
Caller mentions cars nearby; CT ignores it.

items.question: Asked about hazards (gas cylinders, chemicals, power lines)
items.guidance: Identify hazardous materials or utilities that could endanger responders or public.
yes_examples: CT asks about gas, chemicals, power lines, oxygen tanks.
Caller unsure; CT documents 'unknown hazards'.
no_examples: Obvious mention of propane tank ignored.

items.question: Captured ongoing status (spread, smoke, floor, changes)
items.guidance: Obtain and relay significant changes until responders arrive (e.g., spread to roof, explosions).
yes_examples: CT: "Is the fire getting bigger or changing?" Caller: "Flames now on second floor."
CT periodically checks status until units arrive.
no_examples: No follow-up after initial report despite delay.
Fire clearly worsening but CT stops questioning.

sections.name: CAD Skills
sections.section_guidance: Judge by what was actually documented in the CAD/tool_calls, not just spoken.

items.question: Complete fire details entered in CAD
items.guidance: Key elements (location, type, occupants, hazards/exposures if answered) are entered into the record.
yes_examples: CT records location, type, occupants info and hazards in separate CAD fields.
All critical answers appear in update_call_record tool calls.
no_examples: Occupant/hazard info asked but only left in transcript, not in CAD.
CAD has only location; no other critical fields populated.

items.question: Correct priority/status set in CAD
items.guidance: Priority should match SOP (e.g., structure fire = highest).
yes_examples: Set 'Red' or equivalent for working structure fire.
Updated priority when info escalated.
no_examples: Priority never set or left at default.

sections.name: Telephone Protocol / Skills
sections.section_guidance: Evaluate demeanor, control, reassurance, and adherence to procedural steps.

items.question: Dispatch decision followed protocol
items.guidance: Dispatch (or not) according to policy; ensure timeliness (don’t delay critical dispatch).
yes_examples: CT dispatches promptly after core info (address/type).
No dispatch for prank/non-emergency per SOP.
no_examples: Delayed dispatch while asking non-critical questions.
Dispatched when protocol says not to (e.g., clear misuse).

items.question: Maintained control & professional tone
items.guidance: Calm, respectful, gives clear instructions, redirects rambling callers.
yes_examples: CT reassures caller, gives concise directions, stays calm.
Politely but firmly stops prank behavior.
no_examples: CT becomes argumentative or uses slang/sarcasm.
Allows caller to derail call, losing critical info.

sections.name: Interview Questions (Police)
sections.section_guidance: Focus on life-safety first (injuries, weapons, threats), then solvability (descriptions, direction of travel). Judge by transcript content.

items.question: Verified address / location of occurrence
items.guidance: Obtain and confirm exact location; include apartment/unit, landmarks, or intersection as needed.
yes_examples: CT verifies '221B Baker St, Apt 3' with caller.
Caller gives intersection; CT repeats back and confirms.
no_examples: Caller mentions 'near the park' and CT accepts without clarifying.
Address spoken once but never confirmed.

items.question: Correct incident/problem type identified/coded
items.guidance: Match coding to caller’s report (e.g., burglary in progress vs. past theft).
yes_examples: Reports 'man breaking in now' coded as 'B&E in progress'.
Past theft clearly coded 'Theft - Past'.
no_examples: Domestic disturbance coded as 'Noise Complaint' despite mention of assault.

items.question: Caller/complainant name obtained
items.guidance: Request and document the caller’s name; accept initials if full name refused.
yes_examples: CT: 'Your name?' Caller: 'Alex Chen' recorded.
Caller refuses full name; CT records 'Alex (refused surname)'.

items.question: Callback phone number obtained/verified
items.guidance: Ask for and confirm a reachable number; verify auto-populated ANI when appropriate.
yes_examples: CT repeats digits to confirm.
ANI displayed; CT verifies with caller.
no_examples: Digits recorded with obvious error not corrected.

items.question: Asked if weapons are involved / suspect armed
items.guidance: Explicitly ask about weapons and immediate threats to life; document type and location of weapon if known.
yes_examples: 'Are there any weapons involved?' Caller: 'He has a knife.'
CT clarifies location: 'Where is the gun now?'
no_examples: Caller mentions 'gun' but CT does not follow up.

items.question: Suspect/vehicle description and/or direction of travel
items.guidance: Gather descriptors using standard order: person (sex, race, clothing, distinguishing features) and vehicle (make/model/color/plate) and DOT.
yes_examples: Are there any other details or descriptions about the shooter that you can provide? Caller: 'I'm sorry, I don't have a description of the shooter'
'Blue Honda Civic, plate ABC123, headed east on 4th.'
Clothing: 'Black hoodie, jeans, red backpack.'
CT asks about suspect description, but caller refuses to answer.
CT asks about suspect description, but caller cannot answer.
no_examples: Caller describes suspect but CT does not follow up.

sections.name: CAD Skills
sections.section_guidance: Score what is documented in CAD or tool_calls: key fields, priority, officer safety flags, and timely updates.

items.question: Complete relevant info added to CAD (incl. key descriptors)
items.guidance: Location, type, weapon info, suspect/vehicle descriptors captured in appropriate fields.
yes_examples: Weapon details in 'hazards' field; vehicle plate in 'vehicle' field.
DOT captured as a narrative update.
no_examples: Descriptors only in transcript, not in CAD.

items.question: Correct priority/status set in CAD
items.guidance: Crime in progress or threats to life should be high priority per SOP.
yes_examples: Priority escalated upon learning suspect returned.
Set to lower priority for cold report per policy.
no_examples: Left default priority despite active threat.

sections.name: Telephone Protocol / Skills
sections.section_guidance: Evaluate control, safety guidance, and adherence to procedures (e.g., do-not-confront instructions).

items.question: Dispatch decision followed protocol (timely/appropriate)
items.guidance: Dispatch promptly when in-progress or life-safety issues exist; obtain minimal key info first.
yes_examples: Units dispatched after address/type then continued questioning.
No dispatch for non-police matter per policy.
no_examples: Delayed dispatch for descriptors while assault ongoing.

items.question: Maintained control & professional tone throughout
items.guidance: Keep caller safe: advise to avoid confrontation, stay on line when appropriate, use calm tone.
yes_examples: 'Do not approach, stay inside with doors locked.'
CT de-escalates agitated caller.
no_examples: Gives unsafe advice to pursue suspect.

sections.name: Interview Questions (Medical)
sections.section_guidance: Prioritize ABCs (airway, breathing, circulation), chief complaint, and scene safety; judge by transcript content.

items.question: Verified address / location of patient(s)
items.guidance: Confirm exact patient location (unit/entrance code/landmarks).
yes_examples: CT confirms 'Unit 1205, buzzer 1234'.
Repeats rural location landmarks to confirm.
no_examples: Building given but no unit confirmed.

items.question: Chief complaint / problem type correctly identified
items.guidance: Capture concise chief complaint and code appropriately (e.g., chest pain vs. fall).
yes_examples: '55M chest pain' coded as 'Cardiac/Chest Pain'.
Fall with head strike coded as 'Traumatic Injury'.

items.question: Caller name obtained
items.guidance: Request and document caller’s name (or refusal).
yes_examples: Name recorded after polite ask.
Refusal documented as REFUSED.

items.question: Callback phone number obtained/verified
items.guidance: Obtain and verify callback; needed for pre-arrival coaching continuity.
yes_examples: Digits read back to confirm.
ANI verified verbally.

items.question: Asked about consciousness / responsiveness (A&O)
items.guidance: Assess AVPU/A&O where feasible; capture responsiveness and orientation.
yes_examples: 'Is he awake and speaking?'
Clarifies 'only responsive to pain'.

items.question: Asked about breathing / airway status
items.guidance: Determine if breathing is normal/abnormal or absent; identify choking.
yes_examples: 'Is she breathing normally?'
Identifies agonal respirations.

items.question: Assessed for severe bleeding / other life threats
items.guidance: If appropriate, ask about major external bleeding. If obviously no blood due to situation, ask severe pain, stroke signs when relevant.
yes_examples: 'Is there heavy bleeding?'
Screens for FAST stroke signs when slurred speech mentioned.
no_examples: No assessment of obvious life threats.

items.question: Number of patients determined
items.guidance: Establish single vs. multiple patients early for resource sizing.
yes_examples: 'How many people are hurt?'
Clarifies second patient found.

sections.name: CAD Skills
sections.section_guidance: Score documentation: chief complaint, ABC status, priority/determinant, and updates.

items.question: Complete medical info entered in CAD
items.guidance: Chief complaint, consciousness, breathing, bleeding, patient count documented.
yes_examples: ABCs summarized in narrative.
Determinant code captured where used.
no_examples: ABCs asked but missing in CAD.

items.question: Correct priority/status set in CAD
items.guidance: Assign priority per EMD/agency policy (e.g., cardiac arrest = highest).
yes_examples: Upgraded upon report of unresponsive, not breathing.
Downgraded appropriately on minor illness per SOP.
no_examples: Left low priority for respiratory distress.

sections.name: Telephone Protocol / Skills
sections.section_guidance: Evaluate provision of evidence-based pre-arrival instructions (PAIs), reassurance, and re-assessment cadence.

items.question: Provided appropriate pre-arrival care instructions
items.guidance: Deliver PAIs appropriate to complaint (e.g., CPR, bleeding control, airway positioning).
yes_examples: Gives hands-only CPR steps for 'not breathing'.
Advises direct pressure for severe bleeding.

items.question: Reassessed/updated until responders arrived
items.guidance: Monitor changes and give additional instructions while waiting; keep caller on line as appropriate.
yes_examples: Periodic checks on breathing status.
Updates CAD with deteriorations.
no_examples: Hangs up prematurely despite evolving situation.
